what_it_does: Defines a STUB_DEFS object describing six not-yet-built design-system canvases (type, logos, imagery, components, templates, voice) — each with title, subtitle, description, and a feature list — and a StubCanvas React component that renders a placeholder view for a given canvas id: header, an explanatory paragraph, the bullet feature list, and two buttons ('Ask Vi to build this canvas', 'Skip'). Exposes StubCanvas on window.
observations: Six canvas definitions keyed by id: type, logos, imagery, components, templates, voice.
Each def carries title, sub, desc, and a features array of 4 capability descriptions.
StubCanvas looks up the def by id and renders 'Unknown canvas: {id}' if absent.
Placeholder paragraph explicitly says the canvas works 'just like Icons and Colors — gallery on the left, Vi generation, drop-zone for new uploads, applied to the rest of the system automatically.'
Uses CanvasHeader and ViShape components that are not imported here (assumed global from the shared bundle).
Two action buttons: a Vi-AI build button and an outline skip button.
Voice def encodes concrete brand rules in its sub: 'Sentence-case, second-person, no exclamation marks.'
Attached to window.StubCanvas rather than exported as a module.
standouts: The feature lists double as a roadmap/spec for each unbuilt canvas, describing intended Vi-driven behaviours (auto-tagging, off-brand detection, variant generation).
The 'Ask Vi to build this canvas' button frames the placeholder itself as an entry point to generative construction.
conventions: Follows the project's window-global mounting pattern (window.StubCanvas) and relies on globally available shared components rather than ES imports.
Uses dsa-* and feat/features CSS class names consistent with a design-system-app stylesheet.
Centralizes the six stub definitions in one data object, with one component projecting any of them by id — a data-driven render.
concerns: CanvasHeader and ViShape are referenced but not defined/imported in this file; correctness depends on external bundle load order.
One inline style object on the button row (display/gap/justifyContent) rather than a CSS class — a small hardcoded-style deviation from the token/class discipline.
notes: A data-driven placeholder canvas for six design surfaces not yet built; the feature arrays read as the intended spec for each, and a button invites Vi to build them.
questions: Where are CanvasHeader and ViShape defined, and is load order guaranteed before this file runs?
Which surfaces (Icons, Colors) are the 'fully built' reference these stubs promise to mirror?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file's stated purpose (placeholder canvases for surfaces not yet built) is exactly what it implements.
apparent_themes: Scaffolding-as-spec: placeholders that document intended capability.
Vi/AI generation as the path from stub to built surface.
Single design-system surface where every asset class (type, logos, imagery, components, templates, voice) is a parallel canvas.
intention_signals: Aims to make unbuilt surfaces feel coherent and on-roadmap rather than missing, presenting each as a future Vi-generated canvas that mirrors the existing Icons/Colors pattern, with its feature list serving as the build brief.
novelty: The placeholder is itself an invitation to generative build ('Ask Vi to build this canvas'), turning a stub into an actionable construction entry point.
contribution: Contributes the enumerated definitions and intended feature sets for six design-system canvases (type, logos, imagery, components, templates, voice) plus a reusable id-driven placeholder renderer and the brand voice rules embedded in the voice def.
summary_for_embedding: A React file defining six placeholder design-system canvases (type, logos, imagery, components, templates, voice) with feature specs and a data-driven StubCanvas component exposed on window.
intention_for_embedding: To scaffold not-yet-built design surfaces as coherent, on-roadmap placeholders whose feature lists brief their future Vi-generated implementations.
